content.text: I'll read each frame image and judge them against the rubric.

[
  {
    "ruleId": "V1",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "The amber probe and periwinkle candidate cluster is the single focus, with the low-contrast lilac score bars playing a clear supporting role below.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V2",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "The frame presents one coherent widget (probe + four candidates + their four bars), not five-plus independent novel ideas competing at equal weight.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V3",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "Labels c1..c4 sit at each arrow tip, 'probe' rides on the amber arrow, and each score readout is labeled with its candidate name directly beneath the bar; no legend box exists.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V4",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "The only prose is the two-line instruction at top annotating the interactive it sits above; all other text is object labels or numeric readouts.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V5",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "The instruction header, arrow-tip labels, and mono score digits are all legible in every frame, and the 375px frame renders the same text proportionally larger.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V6",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "The arrow cluster, amber range circle, and the full row of four score bars sit entirely inside every frame with margin to the edges.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V7",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "Although 'c3 probe c2' cluster tightly at the top in the initial and length-cheat frames, each word keeps a visible gap and none is rendered illegible by overlap.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V8",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "Amber marks the probe/attention arrow, periwinkle the candidate vectors, lilac the score bars/digits, and cream the labels, held consistent across all five states.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V9",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "The amber ring present in aligned/orthogonal/anti-aligned frames marks the probe's rotation range and carries content; no mascots or filler shapes appear.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V10",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "blocking",
    "evidence": "Each promised state reads from its still: aligned shows c1 peaking at bold 1.0, orthogonal c3 at 1.0 with c1 at 0.0, anti-aligned c1 at -1.0 with its bar below the baseline, and length-cheat shows c1 stretched to a bold 1.3 flipping past c2's 1.0.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V11",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "advisory",
    "evidence": "Score bars sit in a labeled left-to-right row well below the arrow cluster, so identity is preserved despite the vertical gap between an arrow and its bar.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V12",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "advisory",
    "evidence": "The frames form a continuous probe-rotation sequence (40 to 20 to 110 to 200 degrees plus the length stretch) reading as smooth morphs rather than cuts.",
    "suggestion": ""
  },
  {
    "ruleId": "V13",
    "pass": true,
    "severity": "advisory",
    "evidence": "The composition has generous margins and a clear top-to-bottom reading order from instruction to diagram to bars, though a large empty band separates the two groups.",
    "suggestion": ""
  }
]
